Given two points \(A(x_1, y_1)\) and \(B(x_2, y_2)\), the Euclidean distance between them is \(d = \sqrt{(x_2 - x_1)^2 + (y_2 - y_1)^2}\). Among a set of cars indexed by \(i\) at equal time \(t\), each car’s nearest neighbor is the one minimizing this distance \(d_i = \min_{j \ne i} \sqrt{(x_i - x_j)^2 + (y_i - y_j)^2}\).
